meaning: n.爬行,攀缘,抢夺,混乱vi.攀缘,杂乱蔓延,争夺,拼凑,匆忙vt.攀登,搅乱,使混杂
examples: There was an undignified scramble for the best seats.
More than three million fans are expected to scramble for tickets.
She managed to scramble out of the vehicle as it burst into flames.
Further upriver, Marie leads the way in a thrilling scramble though a maze of overground caverns.
Scramble the desks, force them to regroup and continually rewire their brains to know more.
